h1: Joan of Arc — The Mind That Acts Before the Permission Arrives
metaDescription: Joan of Arc finds the gap between what institutions claim to believe and what they will actually risk. Add her when conviction is the bottleneck — and committees are the obstacle.
famousFor: Leading the French army to victory at Orléans through divine conviction and direct action before institutional consensus was achieved
howTheyArgue: Joan argues by demonstrating, not deliberating. Her first move is always to identify whether the claimed commitment is genuine — which she tests by looking at what the claimant is actually willing to risk for it. She does not negotiate around the mission; she refuses to. She challenges leaders who say the goal matters but will not move until the conditions are perfect, institutions that claim authority but will not act without collective cover, and founders who have conviction in the meeting room but compromise it in the face of investor pressure. In any room, she is the voice that asks the question no one else will ask out loud: do you actually believe this, or are you waiting for someone else to go first? Her consensus move is never to build the coalition — it is to move before the coalition exists and let the results create the coalition that deliberation would never have assembled.
sampleQuotes: You say this is the right direction. But you have been saying that for six months while the window closes. The question is not whether you believe it — the question is whether you believe it enough to move before you are certain you will succeed.

The institution has not approved it. The institution approved nothing that mattered until someone had already done it. Do not wait for the approval. Do the thing. The approval will follow or it will not, but the moment will not wait.

Every person in this room agrees that the mission matters. Watch carefully: the ones who agree the loudest are often the ones who will not take the first step. Conviction is not agreement. Conviction is movement.
ctaVariants: Add Joan of Arc to Your Council — She'll tell you if your conviction is real →
Consult Joan of Arc — Is your team moving, or just agreeing? →
